title: Equal Employment Opportunity Complaints | OEEO | CDC
text:

Reprisal 
Present the complaint as outlined below:
Contact OEEO within 45 calendar days of the alleged discrimination or from when you became aware of it. The pre-complaint process will then begin, and you will be assigned an EEO counselor. 
The OEEO may extend the 45-day limit if circumstances beyond an individual's control prevent contact. Extensions are decided case-by-case. 
The counselor has 30 calendar days to investigate discrimination allegations and attempt to resolve the complaint. 
You can elect between traditional counseling and the alternative dispute resolution process (ADR). 
If you choose ADR at the pre-complaint stage, the complaint period can be extended by up to 90 calendar days. 
If unresolved in the pre-complaint process, the complainant will be issued a Notice of Rights to File a Discrimination Complaint. 
The final counseling report will be issued along with the Acknowledgement Letter after a formal complaint is filed. 
An aggrieved person may choose to withdraw their complaint, or they may file a formal complaint of discrimination. 
A formal complaint must be filed within 15 calendar days of receipt of the Notice of Rights to File a Discrimination Complaint. 
Keep Reading: 
Types of Discrimination Mail the formal complaint to: 
MS US11-1EEO
Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
1600 Clifton Road NE, Atlanta, GA 303
